pattern: Locked characters are fixed, unlocked characters are wildcards that can be flipped either way, so a valid assignment exists exactly when a left-to-right scan never runs out of potential opens and a right-to-left scan never runs out of potential closes.
hints: An odd-length string can never be balanced no matter what you flip, so rule that out immediately.
Scan left to right treating every unlocked character as a free '(' when counting how many opens are available so far -- if a locked ')' ever needs more opens than have been seen, it's impossible.
Mirror the same idea scanning right to left, treating unlocked characters as free ')' this time, to make sure there are also never too many opens overall.

edge_inputs.description: odd length string, always impossible regardless of content
fully unlocked and currently unbalanced, but still fixable by flipping
fully unlocked string of even length is always fixable
locked characters force too many opens with no unlocked slots left to compensate
mix of locked and unlocked where the unlocked slots must become closes
smallest possible even length, both unlocked

stress.description: random parentheses and lock patterns at even lengths up to the max size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    if n % 2 == 1:
        n -= 1
    n = max(2, n)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('()') for _ in range(n))
    locked = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [s, locked]
